quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Coal rank"?

options.text: A symbiotic relationship where a parasite benefits by reducing host fitness; can be a significant limiting factor
Classification of coal from lignite to anthracite based on carbon content and energy density, reflecting formation conditions
Total energy captured by producers before respiration losses
The restriction of a species to a particular geographic area, increasing vulnerability to local environmental changes and extinction

quiz_questions.explanation: "Coal rank" is defined as: Classification of coal from lignite to anthracite based on carbon content and energy density, reflecting formation conditions. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Crude oil"?

options.text: The distribution of a population among different age classes, often displayed as a population pyramid
The proportion of soil nutrients in chemical forms accessible to plant roots through absorption
Unrefined petroleum extracted from subsurface deposits, requiring refining into various products (gasoline, diesel, heating oil)
An environmental condition or resource that restricts population growth and prevents populations from reaching theoretical carrying capacity

quiz_questions.explanation: "Crude oil" is defined as: Unrefined petroleum extracted from subsurface deposits, requiring refining into various products (gasoline, diesel, heating oil). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Hydraulic fracturing (fracking)"?

options.text: Technique injecting pressurized fluid to crack rock, enabling extraction of oil and gas from shale formations
Population increase at a constant rate per capita, producing J-shaped growth curve when resources are unlimited
Quantitative assessment of extinction probability incorporating demographic stochasticity and environmental variation
Phenomenon where cities experience higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas from reduced vegetation and heat-absorbing pavement

quiz_questions.explanation: "Hydraulic fracturing (fracking)" is defined as: Technique injecting pressurized fluid to crack rock, enabling extraction of oil and gas from shale formations. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Methane"?

options.text: Continued population growth despite replacement fertility, from age structure weighted toward reproductive years
Harvesting fish faster than populations can reproduce, exceeding sustainable yield and causing population decline
Introduction of harmful substances into soil exceeding background levels and regulatory standards
Primary component of natural gas, a potent greenhouse gas with 28–34 times the 100-year warming potential of CO₂

quiz_questions.explanation: "Methane" is defined as: Primary component of natural gas, a potent greenhouse gas with 28–34 times the 100-year warming potential of CO₂. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Unconventional fossil fuels"?

options.text: Oil and gas from difficult-to-extract sources (tar sands, shale) requiring energy-intensive extraction
The pathway of a chemical element or nutrient through the biotic and abiotic components of an ecosystem
The largest harvest possible indefinitely without reducing stock below level supporting maximum reproduction
Total energy captured by producers before respiration losses

quiz_questions.explanation: "Unconventional fossil fuels" is defined as: Oil and gas from difficult-to-extract sources (tar sands, shale) requiring energy-intensive extraction. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Classification of coal from lignite to anthracite based on carbon content and energy density, reflecting formation conditions"?

options.text: Habitat corridor
Volatile organic compound (VOC)
Doubling time
Coal rank

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Coal rank".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Unrefined petroleum extracted from subsurface deposits, requiring refining into various products (gasoline, diesel, heating oil)"?

options.text: Umbrella species
Density-dependent factor
Crude oil
Carrying capacity (K)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Crude oil".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Technique injecting pressurized fluid to crack rock, enabling extraction of oil and gas from shale formations"?

options.text: Mass extinction
Hydraulic fracturing (fracking)
Biodiversity hotspot
Energy return on investment (EROI)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Hydraulic fracturing (fracking)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Primary component of natural gas, a potent greenhouse gas with 28–34 times the 100-year warming potential of CO₂"?

options.text: Grid integration
Total fertility rate (TFR)
Energy efficiency
Methane

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Methane".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Oil and gas from difficult-to-extract sources (tar sands, shale) requiring energy-intensive extraction"?

options.text: Drought
Age structure
Unconventional fossil fuels
Biodiversity hotspot

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Unconventional fossil fuels".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas is accurate?

options.text: Nuclear Energy generates electricity through controlled fission of uranium-235 or plutonium-239, providing low-carbon, high-density energy but creating long-lived radioactive waste requiring secure isolation for millennia.
The hydrological cycle continuously moves water through evaporation, precipitation, infiltration, and runoff, but human activities increasingly divert water for agriculture, industry, and urban use.

Renewable Energy Sources including solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal, and biomass are replenished on human timescales and produce zero or low lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions compared to fossil fuels.
Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas represent distinct fossil fuel types with different formation, extraction, and combustion characteristics.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas?

options.text: Mining and Land Degradation involve extraction of mineral and energy resources essential for modern society while causing severe environmental degradation including water pollution, habitat destruction, acid drainage, and waste accumulation...
Coal, the most abundant fossil fuel formed from ancient plant material, is extracted through surface and deep mining, providing 27% of global energy but causing severe air and water pollution.
Energy efficiency improvements are constrained by rebound effects where lower energy costs increase demand, partially offsetting efficiency gains.
Understanding energy transfer efficiency is crucial for predicting ecosystem productivity, sustainability, and the impact of human harvesting on food webs.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas?

options.text: Nuclear plants produce 10% of global electricity with high reliability despite public concerns about catastrophic accidents (Chernobyl, Fukushima, Three Mile Island).

Atmosphere and Climate involve the dynamic fluid envelope regulating energy distribution, enabling weather and climate formation, and sustaining life through oxygen, carbon dioxide, and other essential gases.
Oil formed from marine organisms is extracted through conventional and unconventional methods (tar sands, shale oil), driving global transportation.
Effective conservation requires integrating ecological science with economics, policy, and local community engagement.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas?

options.text: Loss of genetic diversity through habitat fragmentation, population bottlenecks, and inbreeding weakens species' long-term survival prospects and reduces the stability of entire ecosystems.
Natural gas, primarily methane, provides cleaner combustion but has significant methane emissions from production and distribution contributing to climate change.
Biomes are large-scale ecosystems classified by climate and dominant vegetation, each providing distinct services shaped by their physical and biological characteristics.
Successful restoration requires understanding the original ecosystem's composition, structure, and ecological processes, then removing limiting factors and facilitating species reestablishment.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas?

options.text: Climate change effects include ecosystem disruption (species range shifts, phenological mismatches), agricultural impacts (yield reductions, new pests), public health threats (heat stress, vector-borne disease expansion), and infrastructure...
Loss of genetic diversity through habitat fragmentation, population bottlenecks, and inbreeding weakens species' long-term survival prospects and reduces the stability of entire ecosystems.
In environmental science, genetic diversity matters because it underpins ecosystem resilience—populations with greater variation are more likely to include individuals that can survive new stressors.
Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas represent distinct fossil fuel types with different formation, extraction, and combustion characteristics.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas?

options.text: Protecting sea turtle populations using structured decision models to balance olive ridley sea turtle harvest needs with population viability
Analyzing how wolf predation limits elk population growth in Yellowstone, affecting vegetation recovery and demonstrating top-down control
Assessing coal seam gas development impacts on groundwater aquifers and agricultural productivity in coal-mining regions
Restoring mountaintop removal mining sites in Appalachia through wildlife habitat creation and carbon sequestration on reclaimed land

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas?

options.text: Establishing predator population recovery following DDT ban in the 1970s, allowing bald eagles and peregrine falcons to rebound
Evaluating tar sands development economic viability and environmental costs including water use, GHG emissions, and habitat disturbance
Phasing out CFC-based refrigerants in cooling systems, replacing with approved alternatives (hydrofluoroolefins, hydrofluoroolefin alternatives, hydrofluorocarbons)
Managing copper mining impacts in Chile's Atacama Desert through water treatment systems addressing acid mine drainage and water depletion

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Using PVA models for Florida panther recovery to determine required population growth rates and habitat connectivity for species recovery
Monitoring methane leakage from natural gas infrastructure to quantify climate impacts and identify equipment upgrades reducing emissions
Understanding snowshoe hare-lynx population cycles where predator-prey dynamics create oscillations through predator lagging and prey recovery
Analyzing how wolf predation limits elk population growth in Yellowstone, affecting vegetation recovery and demonstrating top-down control

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas?

options.text: Assessing coal seam gas development impacts on groundwater aquifers and agricultural productivity in coal-mining regions
Projecting Japan's shrinking population using an inverted age pyramid to anticipate workforce shortages and retirement system challenges
Accounting for material footprint of goods (mining waste, energy), prompting consumption reduction and circular economy transitions
Implementing stormwater pollution prevention through retention ponds, rain gardens, and permeable pavements in urban development

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas in practice?

options.text: Decommissioning aging nuclear plants through radioactive material removal, facility decontamination, and site remediation
Restoring mined lands by deliberately introducing pioneer species and managing succession toward productive ecosystems or wildlife habitat
Evaluating tar sands development economic viability and environmental costs including water use, GHG emissions, and habitat disturbance
Managing forest successional pathways after logging or fire by controlling which species regenerate to achieve desired forest composition

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Coal, Oil, and Natural Gas.
